title: cipadessa baccifera var. cinerascens
intro: Cipadessa baccifera var. cinerascens, also known as the Ceylon tea tree, is a variety of the Cipadessa baccifera plant, native to Sri Lanka and parts of India, which is a species of flowering plant in the family Staphylocarpaceae. The Ceylon tea tree has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cipadessa baccifera var. cinerascens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Ceylon tea tree are used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
